status.loadingSummary: Loading runtime configuration, layers, and briefings.
status.missingConfig: The lab runtime configuration is not available.

hero.actionsSummary: Quick access rail for the first tasks once the lab is already running.
hero.actionsTitle: Open routing, inspect topology, or install the gateway certificate.

sections.aiBodyDisabled: The AI layer remains optional. When it is off, the dashboard shows the exact command required to enable it.
sections.aiBodyEnabled: The AI layer is online: the dashboard takes you into Open WebUI or the Ollama APIs with credentials and operating context always visible.
sections.footerBody: Four quick signals on routing, persistence, recommended usage, and layer segmentation.
sections.imageBodyDisabled: The image generation layer stays off until you explicitly request it. When inactive, the dashboard shows the exact activation command.
sections.imageBodyEnabled: The image generation layer is online: InvokeAI, SwarmUI, and Fooocus are exposed through dedicated gateways, while FLUX.2 klein 4B and Qwen-Image-2512 stay pre-seeded for the paired runtimes.
sections.servicesBody: The core plane is always available. Each card collects endpoints, operational credentials, and a direct entry point into the service.
sections.workbenchBodyDisabled: The environments stay detached from the core. You enable them only when you actually need code-server or shared Postgres.
sections.workbenchBodyEnabled: The environments are active: you can jump directly into a workspace or open its dedicated briefing first to review networking, credentials, and tools.

briefing.contentServedByRuntime: Content served by the Atlas runtime.

errors.invalidRuntimeObject: Runtime configuration is missing or invalid.
errors.runtimeHtmlFallback: Runtime config is unavailable: start Atlas Lab through the gateway or use npm run dev:atlas-dashboard.

dashboard.coreLayerSummary: Gitea and n8n remain the always-on plane of the lab.

accessNotes.aiDisabled: The AI layer no longer starts by default: the deck marks it as optional instead of pretending that it is online.
accessNotes.aiEnabled: Open WebUI and Ollama are really online and reachable on the AI gateway ports.
accessNotes.credentials: Operational credentials are exposed here and remain aligned with the lab bootstrap.
accessNotes.https: All browser ingresses use localhost with dedicated HTTPS, without custom DNS or hosts-file edits.
accessNotes.imageDisabled: The image generation layer remains optional and no dedicated image endpoint is exposed until you enable it.
accessNotes.imageEnabled: InvokeAI, SwarmUI, and Fooocus are online behind dedicated gateways. FLUX.2 klein 4B and Qwen-Image-2512 are already staged, while Fooocus keeps its own persistent data workspace.
accessNotes.n8n: n8n logs in directly with the bootstrapped owner account: there is no second gateway login.
accessNotes.workbenchDisabled: Workbench and Postgres stay separated from the core operating plane until you enable the dedicated layer.
accessNotes.workbenchEnabled: Workbench and Postgres are active: you can open browser-based environments or connect from the desktop to the host Postgres port.

aiLayer.description: Optional AI layer for local conversational workflows and GPU-backed LLM inference. The deck enables it only when you explicitly request it.
aiLayer.summaryDisabled: AI layer is off. No AI service is started or exposed until you enable the dedicated flag.
aiLayer.summaryEnabled: Open WebUI and Ollama are active and served through the AI gateway.
aiLayer.title: AI Layer

ollama.description: Secure gateway to the local Ollama endpoint. It serves LLMs and embedding models with GPU acceleration from the host node.
ollama.title: Ollama Core

openWebUi.description: Conversational interface for working with local lab models in the browser, with explicit credentials and immediate access from the gateway.
openWebUi.title: Open WebUI

imageLayer.description: Optional image generation layer for local visual workflows. It exposes InvokeAI, SwarmUI, and Fooocus through dedicated gateways and stages FLUX.2 klein 4B plus Qwen-Image-2512 in persistent storage for the paired runtimes.
imageLayer.summaryDisabled: Image layer is off. InvokeAI, SwarmUI, and Fooocus are not started or exposed until you enable the dedicated flag.
imageLayer.summaryEnabled: InvokeAI, SwarmUI, and Fooocus are active behind the image gateways, with persistent model and output storage kept inside Docker volumes.
imageLayer.title: Image Layer

fooocus.description: Prompt-first image workspace based on the official Fooocus container, served through the dedicated image gateway.
fooocus.note: Fooocus keeps its config, downloaded models, and generated outputs in a persistent Docker volume and may fetch additional assets during use.
fooocus.title: Fooocus Flow

invokeAi.description: Browser-based image generation console served through the dedicated image gateway with a pre-seeded FLUX.2 klein 4B model.
invokeAi.note: Model source: {{modelRepo}}. The model is staged into persistent storage and scanned by InvokeAI at startup.
invokeAi.title: InvokeAI Studio

swarmUi.description: Browser-based image workflow console backed by a self-starting ComfyUI runtime and paired with the Qwen-Image-2512 FP8 model.
swarmUi.note: Model source: {{modelRepo}}. SwarmUI prepares its self-starting ComfyUI backend and loads the staged Qwen model from persistent storage.
swarmUi.title: SwarmUI Console

persistence.body: Databases, workspaces, models, certificates, and runtime configuration stay persisted only in named Docker volumes.
persistence.label: persistence

routing.body: The deck stays on {{localUrl}}; browser services go through the HTTPS gateway while Postgres for desktop clients uses {{postgresHost}}:{{postgresPort}}.
routing.label: routing

segmentation.body: Internal networks remain split for edge, AI, data services, and workbenches. The gateways are the only published entry points.
segmentation.label: segmentation

usage.body: Gitea and n8n are the always-on core plane; AI, image generation, and workbench layers are enabled only when they are actually needed.
usage.label: usage

quickActions.localDeckDescription: Review from the browser the local routing the deck is currently using.
quickActions.networkMapDescription: Read the lab topology and the published network planes.

hero.summary: Unified control room for repository work, automation, optional AI, image generation, and development environments. Browser ports stay on HTTPS over localhost, while Postgres from the workbench layer also exposes a host-side TCP port.

aiEnabled.caption: AI layer published on the dedicated gateway
aiEnabled.label: ai live

imageEnabled.caption: InvokeAI, SwarmUI, and Fooocus published on dedicated image gateways
imageEnabled.label: image live

workbenchEnabled.caption: workspaces and database exposed in the dedicated layer
workbenchEnabled.label: workbench live

operatingCharter.ai: Open WebUI and Ollama live in an explicitly optional AI layer.
operatingCharter.gitea: Gitea governs code, review, and Git governance for the project.
operatingCharter.image: InvokeAI, SwarmUI, and Fooocus live in a dedicated image-generation layer with FLUX.2 klein 4B and Qwen-Image-2512 support.
operatingCharter.n8n: n8n coordinates automations, webhooks, and operational jobs in the lab.
operatingCharter.workbench: Code-server and Postgres stay in a workbench plane separated from the core.

gitea.description: Internal Git forge for repositories, issues, review, and the lab's technical collaboration flow.
gitea.title: Gitea Forge

n8n.description: Orchestration engine for webhooks, integrations, and operational pipelines, already bootstrapped with an application owner account.
n8n.note: The first access skips the setup wizard but still exposes the official templates available inside the application.
n8n.title: n8n Automation

workbenchLayer.description: Separate layer for browser-based workspaces and a shared database. The core stays clean until you truly need the development environment.
workbenchLayer.summaryDisabled: Workbench layer is off. No code-server or Postgres is exposed by the lab.
workbenchLayer.summaryEnabled: Node, Python, AI, C++, and Postgres are online in the workbench plane.
workbenchLayer.title: Workbench Layer

ai.description: Code-server workspace for notebooks, ML experiments, and local AI tooling.
ai.title: AI Reactor

cpp.description: Code-server workspace for C or C++ development, native compilation, and debugging.
cpp.title: C++ Foundry

node.description: Code-server workspace for JavaScript, TypeScript, frontend work, and npm or pnpm tooling.
node.title: Node Forge

postgres.description: Shared PostgreSQL for schema design, local queries, migrations, and relational testing from the desktop or from containers.
postgres.note: From the host system use localhost with the desktop port. Inside containers, keep using postgres-dev:5432.
postgres.title: Postgres Vault

python.description: Code-server workspace for Python backend work, scripting, and operational automations.
python.title: Python Grid
